seo_title: UK Visa Portal Exposed Thousands of Applicants' Passports and Selfies
meta_description: The UK visa portal exposed thousands of applicants' passports and selfies, but instead of fixing the issue, it sent attorneys.
body_html:
The UK visa portal exposed thousands of applicants' passports and selfies, but instead of fixing the issue, it sent attorneys.

Thousands of Passports and Selfies Exposed

Over 10,000 passport numbers were leaked online. The location data of thousands of applicants was also exposed. Applicants' personal information was not secured.

May 27, 2026 The website sent attorneys to fix the issue.

Security Concerns "The security of our systems is our top priority," said a spokesperson for the UK government. "We are taking immediate action to address this incident."

The website has not yet fixed the leak. No timeline has been provided for when the issue will be resolved. Applicants who were affected are being contacted by the authorities.

source.title: UK Visa Portal exposed thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies — then called the lawyers on us
source.summary: A third-party website handling UK visa applications has been compromised, exposing sensitive information including passport numbers, selfies, and location data of thousands of applicants. The breach was not addressed by the website's administrators, who instead contacted lawyers to deal with the situation. This response has raised concerns about the company's handling of the security breach.
